acceptance_criteria:
Acceptance criteria:
- Cache invalidates on config file changes, routes.jsonl changes, redirects, CLI overrides, and DB path changes.
- No unsafe code and no torn semantic reads; optimistic retry falls back to direct discovery.
- Startup syscall/timing benchmark demonstrates a win before enabling by default.
- Tests cover local, routed, stale, and concurrently-mutated metadata cases.
description: Reduce repeated startup filesystem and config work by adding an explicitly invalidated route/config cache with optimistic version checks. The alien-graveyard inspiration is seqlocks: very cheap reads, retry or fallback on concurrent writes. The Rust implementation should stay safe and simple; no unsafe code. Tests: config mutation invalidation, routed workspace changes, stale-cache fallback, and startup syscall benchmarks.
title: Add optimistic route/config cache for startup metadata
